---
title: "MP112: Access Control and Authorization"
subtitle: "Role-based access control and principle of least privilege"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "meta-principle"
number: "MP112"
date-created: "2025-08-30"
date-modified: "2025-08-30"
author: "Principle Revisor"
type: "security-meta-principle"
law: "Access Control and Authorization Law"
article-number: 
  number: 112
  title: "Access Control and Authorization Framework"
influences:
  - "MP009: Default Deny"
  - "MP031: Separation of Concerns"
  - "MP110: Security Credentials Management"
  - "MP111: Data Privacy Protection"
extends:
  - "MP009: Default Deny Principle"
criticality: "CRITICAL"
enforcement: "MANDATORY"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
---

## Core Statement

All system access MUST be controlled through a comprehensive Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) framework implementing the principle of least privilege, with explicit authorization required for every resource access, strong authentication mechanisms, and complete audit trails.

## Authorization Framework

### Principle of Least Privilege

Every user, process, and system component must operate with the minimum set of privileges necessary to accomplish their legitimate functions. No more, no less.

```r
# CORRECT - Least privilege implementation
grant_minimum_access <- function(user_role, resource) {
  required_permissions <- get_required_permissions(user_role, resource)
  grant_permissions(user_role, required_permissions)
  log_access_grant(user_role, resource, required_permissions)
}

# VIOLATION - Excessive privileges
grant_admin_to_everyone <- function() {
  # NEVER DO THIS - Violates least privilege
  grant_permissions("all_users", "admin")  # CRITICAL VIOLATION
}
```

## Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

### Role Hierarchy

```yaml
# roles_hierarchy.yaml
roles:
  super_admin:
    level: 0
    inherits: []
    description: "System administration only"
    
  admin:
    level: 1
    inherits: [data_manager]
    description: "Application administration"
    
  data_manager:
    level: 2
    inherits: [analyst]
    description: "Data management and configuration"
    
  analyst:
    level: 3
    inherits: [viewer]
    description: "Data analysis and reporting"
    
  viewer:
    level: 4
    inherits: []
    description: "Read-only access"
    
  guest:
    level: 5
    inherits: []
    description: "Minimal public access"
```

### Permission Matrix

```r
# Permission definitions
permission_matrix <- list(
  # Resource: Database
  database = list(
    super_admin = c("create", "read", "update", "delete", "admin"),
    admin = c("read", "update", "delete"),
    data_manager = c("read", "update"),
    analyst = c("read"),
    viewer = c("read_limited"),
    guest = c()  # No database access
  ),
  
  # Resource: Reports
  reports = list(
    super_admin = c("create", "read", "update", "delete", "publish"),
    admin = c("create", "read", "update", "delete", "publish"),
    data_manager = c("create", "read", "update", "publish"),
    analyst = c("create", "read", "update"),
    viewer = c("read"),
    guest = c("read_public")
  ),
  
  # Resource: User Management
  user_management = list(
    super_admin = c("create", "read", "update", "delete", "assign_roles"),
    admin = c("create", "read", "update", "assign_roles"),
    data_manager = c("read"),
    analyst = c(),
    viewer = c(),
    guest = c()
  )
)
```

## Authentication Implementation

### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

```r
# MFA requirement enforcement
require_mfa <- function(user, resource_sensitivity) {
  mfa_required <- resource_sensitivity >= "confidential" ||
                  user$role %in% c("admin", "super_admin", "data_manager")
  
  if (mfa_required) {
    if (!verify_mfa(user)) {
      log_security_event("MFA_REQUIRED", user$id, resource_sensitivity)
      stop("Multi-factor authentication required for this resource")
    }
  }
  
  return(TRUE)
}

# MFA verification
verify_mfa <- function(user) {
  # Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP)
  totp_valid <- verify_totp(user$totp_token, user$secret)
  
  # Backup: SMS or Email code
  if (!totp_valid) {
    backup_code <- request_backup_code(user)
    return(verify_backup_code(user, backup_code))
  }
  
  return(totp_valid)
}
```

### Session Management

```r
# Secure session creation
create_secure_session <- function(user, ip_address) {
  session <- list(
    id = generate_secure_token(32),
    user_id = user$id,
    role = user$role,
    created_at = Sys.time(),
    expires_at = Sys.time() + hours(8),  # 8-hour max session
    ip_address = ip_address,
    last_activity = Sys.time()
  )
  
  # Secure session storage
  store_session_secure(session)
  
  # Set secure cookie
  set_cookie(
    name = "session_id",
    value = session$id,
    httponly = TRUE,
    secure = TRUE,
    samesite = "Strict",
    max_age = 8 * 3600
  )
  
  return(session)
}

# Session validation
validate_session <- function(session_id, ip_address) {
  session <- get_session(session_id)
  
  # Multiple validation checks
  validations <- list(
    exists = !is.null(session),
    not_expired = session$expires_at > Sys.time(),
    ip_match = session$ip_address == ip_address,
    not_idle = Sys.time() - session$last_activity < minutes(30)
  )
  
  if (!all(unlist(validations))) {
    invalidate_session(session_id)
    log_security_event("SESSION_INVALID", session_id, validations)
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  # Update last activity
  update_session_activity(session_id)
  return(TRUE)
}
```

## Authorization Engine

### Resource-Level Authorization

```r
# Fine-grained authorization checks
authorize_resource_access <- function(user, resource, action) {
  # Get user's effective permissions
  user_permissions <- get_effective_permissions(user$role)
  
  # Check resource-specific permissions
  resource_permissions <- user_permissions[[resource$type]]
  
  # Verify action is allowed
  if (!(action %in% resource_permissions)) {
    log_authorization_failure(user$id, resource$id, action)
    stop(sprintf("Unauthorized: User %s cannot %s resource %s",
                 user$id, action, resource$id))
  }
  
  # Additional context-based checks
  if (!check_contextual_authorization(user, resource, action)) {
    stop("Contextual authorization failed")
  }
  
  log_authorization_success(user$id, resource$id, action)
  return(TRUE)
}

# Contextual authorization
check_contextual_authorization <- function(user, resource, action) {
  checks <- list(
    # Time-based access
    time_allowed = is_within_allowed_hours(user$role),
    
    # Location-based access
    location_allowed = is_allowed_location(user$ip_address),
    
    # Data ownership
    owns_resource = resource$owner_id == user$id ||
                   user$role %in% c("admin", "super_admin"),
    
    # Department isolation
    department_match = user$department == resource$department ||
                      user$role %in% c("admin", "super_admin")
  )
  
  return(all(unlist(checks)))
}
```

### Dynamic Permission Evaluation

```r
# Runtime permission evaluation
evaluate_dynamic_permissions <- function(user, context) {
  base_permissions <- get_role_permissions(user$role)
  
  # Apply dynamic rules
  dynamic_permissions <- base_permissions
  
  # Temporal restrictions
  if (!is_business_hours() && user$role == "analyst") {
    dynamic_permissions <- remove_permission(dynamic_permissions, "export")
  }
  
  # Conditional elevation
  if (context$emergency_mode && user$certified_responder) {
    dynamic_permissions <- add_permission(dynamic_permissions, "emergency_access")
  }
  
  # Delegation
  if (context$delegated_from) {
    delegated_perms <- get_delegated_permissions(user$id, context$delegated_from)
    dynamic_permissions <- merge_permissions(dynamic_permissions, delegated_perms)
  }
  
  return(dynamic_permissions)
}
```

## Access Control Lists (ACLs)

```r
# ACL implementation for fine-grained control
manage_acl <- function(resource_id, principal_id, permission, action = "grant") {
  acl_entry <- list(
    resource_id = resource_id,
    principal_id = principal_id,
    principal_type = detect_principal_type(principal_id),
    permission = permission,
    granted_by = get_current_user(),
    granted_at = Sys.time()
  )
  
  if (action == "grant") {
    add_acl_entry(acl_entry)
    log_acl_change("GRANT", acl_entry)
  } else if (action == "revoke") {
    remove_acl_entry(resource_id, principal_id, permission)
    log_acl_change("REVOKE", acl_entry)
  }
}

# Check ACL permissions
check_acl <- function(user_id, resource_id, required_permission) {
  # Get all applicable ACL entries
  acl_entries <- get_acl_entries(resource_id)
  
  # Check direct user permissions
  user_acls <- filter(acl_entries, principal_id == user_id)
  if (required_permission %in% user_acls$permission) return(TRUE)
  
  # Check group permissions
  user_groups <- get_user_groups(user_id)
  group_acls <- filter(acl_entries, principal_id %in% user_groups)
  if (required_permission %in% group_acls$permission) return(TRUE)
  
  return(FALSE)
}
```

## Privilege Escalation Prevention

```r
# Prevent unauthorized privilege escalation
prevent_privilege_escalation <- function(user, requested_role) {
  current_level <- get_role_level(user$role)
  requested_level <- get_role_level(requested_role)
  
  # Cannot grant higher privileges than you have
  if (requested_level < current_level) {
    log_security_event("PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_ATTEMPT", user$id, requested_role)
    alert_security_team(user$id, "Privilege escalation attempt")
    stop("Cannot grant privileges higher than your own role")
  }
  
  # Cannot modify your own privileges
  if (user$id == target_user_id) {
    log_security_event("SELF_PRIVILEGE_MODIFICATION", user$id)
    stop("Cannot modify your own privileges")
  }
  
  # Require approval for sensitive role changes
  if (requested_role %in% c("admin", "super_admin")) {
    require_approval(user$id, target_user_id, requested_role)
  }
}
```

## API Security

```r
# API key management
manage_api_keys <- function(user_id, action, key_id = NULL) {
  if (action == "create") {
    api_key <- list(
      key_id = generate_uuid(),
      key_value = generate_secure_token(32),
      user_id = user_id,
      created_at = Sys.time(),
      expires_at = Sys.time() + days(90),
      permissions = get_api_permissions(user_id),
      rate_limit = get_rate_limit(user_id)
    )
    
    store_api_key(hash_key(api_key$key_value), api_key)
    return(api_key$key_value)  # Return only once
    
  } else if (action == "revoke") {
    revoke_api_key(key_id)
    log_api_key_event("REVOKED", key_id, user_id)
  }
}

# API authentication
authenticate_api_request <- function(request) {
  api_key <- extract_api_key(request$headers)
  
  if (is.null(api_key)) {
    return(list(authenticated = FALSE, reason = "Missing API key"))
  }
  
  key_data <- validate_api_key(hash_key(api_key))
  
  if (!key_data$valid) {
    log_api_auth_failure(api_key, request$ip)
    return(list(authenticated = FALSE, reason = key_data$reason))
  }
  
  # Check rate limits
  if (!check_rate_limit(key_data$key_id, request$ip)) {
    return(list(authenticated = FALSE, reason = "Rate limit exceeded"))
  }
  
  return(list(
    authenticated = TRUE,
    user_id = key_data$user_id,
    permissions = key_data$permissions
  ))
}
```

## Audit and Compliance

```r
# Comprehensive audit logging
log_access_event <- function(event_type, user_id, resource_id, action, result) {
  audit_log <- list(
    timestamp = Sys.time(),
    event_type = event_type,
    user_id = user_id,
    session_id = get_current_session_id(),
    resource_id = resource_id,
    action = action,
    result = result,
    ip_address = get_client_ip(),
    user_agent = get_user_agent(),
    additional_context = capture_context()
  )
  
  # Write to immutable audit log
  write_audit_log(audit_log)
  
  # Real-time alerting for suspicious activity
  if (is_suspicious_activity(audit_log)) {
    alert_security_team(audit_log)
  }
}

# Access review automation
conduct_access_review <- function() {
  # Identify excessive permissions
  excessive_permissions <- identify_excessive_permissions()
  
  # Find dormant accounts
  dormant_accounts <- find_dormant_accounts(days = 90)
  
  # Check role assignments
  inappropriate_roles <- validate_role_assignments()
  
  # Generate review report
  review_report <- generate_access_review_report(
    excessive_permissions,
    dormant_accounts,
    inappropriate_roles
  )
  
  # Automatic remediation for clear violations
  auto_remediate_access_issues(review_report)
  
  return(review_report)
}
```

## Integration Requirements

### Shiny Application Integration

```r
# Shiny server authentication
shinyServer(function(input, output, session) {
  # Check authentication on startup
  user <- reactive({
    validate_session_or_redirect(session)
  })
  
  # Protect sensitive outputs
  output$sensitive_data <- renderTable({
    req(authorize_resource_access(user(), "sensitive_data", "read"))
    get_sensitive_data()
  })
  
  # Protect actions
  observeEvent(input$delete_button, {
    req(authorize_resource_access(user(), "records", "delete"))
    delete_records(input$selected_ids)
  })
})
```

## Related Rules and Principles

### Derived Security Rules
- **R220**: RBAC implementation standards
- **R221**: Session timeout configuration
- **R222**: MFA enforcement policies
- **R223**: API key rotation schedule
- **R224**: Access review frequency

### Integration with Other MPs
- **MP110**: Security Credentials Management
- **MP111**: Data Privacy Protection
- **MP115**: Audit Logging and Monitoring
- **MP118**: Development Security Practices

## Compliance Requirements

- **SOC 2**: Access control requirements
- **ISO 27001**: Access management controls
- **NIST 800-53**: Access control family
- **PCI DSS**: Requirement 7 - Restrict access

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] RBAC roles defined and documented
- [ ] Least privilege implemented
- [ ] MFA enabled for sensitive access
- [ ] Session management secure
- [ ] API authentication implemented
- [ ] Audit logging comprehensive
- [ ] Access reviews scheduled
- [ ] Privilege escalation prevented
- [ ] Authorization checks consistent

---

**Security through control**: Every access must be authenticated, authorized, and audited. Trust nothing, verify everything, log all activities.
